name: Extreme Ask Concentration (Contra Z-Score)
hypothesis: The predictive power of ask-side liquidity concentration is most pronounced in its statistical extremes. A very high concentration relative to its recent history (high z-score) signals strong resistance, leading to a price drop. Therefore, a very negative z-score should predict a price rise.
mutation_note: Following feedback to test extreme quantiles, this spec replaces the static threshold of the parent with a dynamic z-score threshold (z > 2.5) to isolate the signal's tail. The contra-directional nature (`long_if_neg`) is preserved.
